title: Other National Rebellions
summary: From Ly Bi and Mai Thuc Loan to Phung Hung... the persistent and tireless flames of struggle of our people.

slides.text: In the 6th century, Ly Bi led an uprising to drive out the Liang army and established the state of Van Xuan - hoping the country would be as beautiful as an eternal spring.
slides.imagePrompt: Ly Bi (Ly Nam De) being crowned, Vạn Xuân flag, festive atmosphere, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Following him were Mai Thuc Loan (Mai Hac De) in Nghe An and Phung Hung (Bo Cai Dai Vuong) in Duong Lam.
slides.imagePrompt: Collage of different heroes leading their people, determined expressions, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Each uprising, whether successful or not, contributed more strength to the Vietnamese nation's desire for independence.
slides.imagePrompt: A large bonfire where stories of past heroes are passed down, glowing energy, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: This period is the best proof of the saying: 'When invaders come, even women fight,' and of our people's never-fading patriotism.
slides.imagePrompt: Symbolic image of diverse Vietnamese people (men, women, children) standing together in solidarity, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: What state did Ly Bi establish?
What title did our people give to Phung Hung?
